body_txt: A drawn facial expression to show that a character is nervous, embarrassed, scared, or happy by having their mouth form into a curved zig-zag/squiggly line. When used to express negative emotions, a character’s mouth may curve into a squiggly frown. it’s often accompanied by an Overly Nervous Flop Sweat or a Sweat Drop ; they might dart their eyes back and forth as their body trembles, while excessively biting their nails to signify them being nervous or afraid. Alternatively, it might be used to show a character feeling nauseous or sometimes sleepy . Often associated with characters who are nervous wrecks , shrinking violets , or butt-monkeys . When used to express positive emotions, a character’s mouth may curve upwards to make a squiggly smiley face. Usually used to show a character holding in their laughter , or having a goofy grin. Might be accompanied by squinted eyes, their eyes turning into hearts , or little hearts floating around them to indicate them falling in love. Can be associated with plucky comic reliefs or giggly characters. Other examples include eyes turning into spirals , becoming cross-eyed, or having their pupils spin in opposite directions to indicate them being dizzy, confused, or sometimes crazy. Might be accompanied by birds or stars circling around their heads , or scratching their head with their index finger. Can be associated with ditzy characters , clumsy characters , or cloudcuckoolanders . Most commonly seen in Comic Strips , Western Animation , Anime , and Manga . Might overlap with Holding in Laughter . Compare Quivering Lip , when a character’s lips quiver into a moving squiggly line before crying. Also compare Playful Cat Smile , a mouth expression used for cuteness or playfulness.
